Name: Hoppip
Flavor-Text:
Outside of cold regions, Hoppip can be found practically everywhere.
This is because the wind carries this species all over the world.

Name: Skiploom
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Skiploom enthusiasts can apparently tell where a Skiploom was born by the scent drifting from the flower on the Pokémon’s head.

Name: Jumpluff ex
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Sunkern
Flavor-Text:
It suddenly falls out of the sky in the morning.
Knowing it’s weak, it simply feeds until it evolves.

Name: Sunflora
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: In the daytime, it rushes about in a hectic manner, but it comes to a complete stop when the sun sets.

Name: Celebi
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an evolved Pokémon, devolve it by putting the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent's hand.
Flavor-Text:
This Pokémon wanders across time.
Grass and trees flourish in the forests in which it has appeared.

Name: Durant
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.
Flavor-Text: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves from Heatmor.

Name: Slugma
Flavor-Text: A common sight in volcanic areas, it slowly slithers around in a constant search for warm places.

Name: Magcargo
Flavor-Text: Its brittle shell occasionally spouts intense flames that circulate throughout its body.

Name: Entei ex
Ability-Effect: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw a card.
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [R] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Fletchinder
Flavor-Text: Fletchinder scatters embers in tall grass where bug Pokémon might be hiding and then catches them as they come leaping out.

Name: Talonflame
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text:
It has top-notch flying capabilities.
It flies around easily, even while carrying prey that weighs more than 220 lbs.

Name: Poliwag
Effects: Put 1 random Poliwag from your deck onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text:
For Poliwag, swimming is easier than walking.
The swirl pattern on its belly is actually part of the Pokémon’s innards showing through the skin.

Name: Poliwhirl
Flavor-Text:
Staring at the swirl on its belly causes drowsiness.
This trait of Poliwhirl’s has been used in place of lullabies to get children to go to sleep.

Name: Tentacool
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text:
Tentacool is not a particularly strong swimmer.
It drifts across the surface of shallow seas as it searches for prey.

Name: Tentacruel
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned and Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text:
When the red orbs on Tentacruel’s head glow brightly, watch out.
The Pokémon is about to fire off a burst of ultrasonic waves.

Name: Slowpoke
Flavor-Text:
It is incredibly slow and dopey.
It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.

Name: Slowking
Effects:
Discard up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards from your hand.
This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: When its head was bitten, toxins entered Slowpoke’s head and unlocked an extraordinary power.

Name: Jynx
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text:
Its strange cries sound like human language.
There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

Name: Suicune ex
Ability-Effect: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw a card.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Feebas
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Although unattractive and unpopular, this Pokémon’s marvelous vitality has made it a subject of research.

Name: Milotic
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may heal 60 damage from 1 of your [W] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Milotic has provided inspiration to many artists.
It has even been referred to as the most beautiful Pokémon of all.

Name: Mantyke
Effects: Take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Basic Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
It’s highly friendly and easily tamed.
Tours that take people swimming with Mantyke are a super-popular beach activity.

Name: Cryogonal
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Cryogonal appear during cold seasons.
It is said that people and Pokémon who die on snowy mountains are reborn into these Pokémon.

Name: Raikou ex
Ability-Effect: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw a card.
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Tynamo
Flavor-Text: While one alone doesn’t have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

Name: Eelektrik
Flavor-Text: They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

Name: Eelektross
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text:
They crawl out of the ocean using their arms.
They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

Name: Stunfisk
Flavor-Text: Thanks to bacteria that lived in the mud flats with it, this Pokémon developed the organs it uses to generate electricity.

Name: Yamper
Flavor-Text:
This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats.
As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

Name: Boltund
Effects: If this Pokémon has damage on it, this attack can be used for 1 [L] Energy.
Flavor-Text:
This Pokémon generates electricity and channels it into its legs to keep them going strong.
Boltund can run nonstop for three full days.

Name: Misdreavus
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may have your opponent reveal their hand.
Flavor-Text:
This Pokémon startles people in the middle of the night.
It gathers fear as its energy.

Name: Mismagius
Effects: At the end of your opponent's next turn, do 90 damage to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Its cry sounds like an incantation.
It is said the cry may rarely be imbued with happiness-giving power.

Name: Galarian Corsola
Flavor-Text:
Watch your step when wandering areas oceans once covered.
What looks like a stone could be this Pokémon, and it will curse you if you kick it.

Name: Galarian Cursola
Ability-Effect:
If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, flip a coin.
If heads, the Attacking Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text:
Its shell is overflowing with its heightened otherworldly energy.
The ectoplasm serves as protection for this Pokémon’s core spirit.

Name: Latias
Effects: If Latios is on your Bench, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
It communicates using telepathy.
Its body is covered in down that refracts light to make it invisible.

Name: Latios
Ability-Effect: If you have Latias in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
It understands human speech and is highly intelligent.
It is a tender Pokémon that dislikes fighting.

Name: Frillish
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It envelops its prey in its veillike arms and draws it down to the deeps, five miles below the ocean’s surface.

Name: Jellicent
Flavor-Text:
Most of this Pokémon’s body composition is identical to sea water.
It makes sunken ships its lair.

Name: Diglett
Flavor-Text:
It lives about one yard underground, where it feeds on plant roots.
It sometimes appears aboveground.

Name: Dugtrio
Effects:
Discard the top card of your deck.
If that card is a [F] Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its three heads bob separately up and down to loosen the soil nearby, making it easier for it to burrow.

Name: Poliwrath ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [W] Energy attached, this attack does 40 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Phanpy
Effects: This attack does damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon equal to the damage this Pokémon has on it.
Flavor-Text:
This Pokémon lives and nests on a riverbank.
After playing in the mud, it won’t be able to settle down unless it washes its body.

Name: Donphan
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text:
Donphan is covered in tough hide, so even being hit by a car won’t faze this Pokémon.
However, it is extremely susceptible to rain.

Name: Relicanth
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Rock-hard scales and oil-filled swim bladders allow this Pokémon to survive the intense water pressure of the deep sea.

Name: Dwebble
Flavor-Text:
It makes a hole in a suitable rock.
If that rock breaks, the Pokémon remains agitated until it locates a replacement.

Name: Crustle
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
This highly territorial Pokémon prefers dry climates.
It won’t come out of its boulder on rainy days.

Name: Seviper
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Zangoose, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
Constant polishing makes the edge of the blade on its tail extremely sharp.
It’s Zangoose’s archrival.

Name: Zorua
Flavor-Text:
It changes into the forms of others to surprise them.
Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

Name: Zoroark
Ability-Effect: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this Pokémon's attacks, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Stories say those who tried to catch Zoroark were trapped in an illusion and punished.

Name: Inkay
Flavor-Text:
It spins while making its luminescent spots flash.
These spots allow it to communicate with others by using different patterns of light.

Name: Malamar
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Gazing at its luminescent spots will quickly induce a hypnotic state, putting the observer under Malamar’s control.

Name: Skrelp
Flavor-Text:
Skrelp evades its enemies by hiding amid drifting seaweed.
It eats rotten seaweed to create its poison.

Name: Dragalge
Flavor-Text:
Using a liquid poison, Dragalge indiscriminately attacks anything that wanders into its territory.
This poison can corrode the undersides of boats.

Name: Altaria
Effects: If this Pokémon has 2 or more different types of Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: If it bonds with a person, it will gently envelop the friend with its soft wings, then hum.

Name: Farfetch'd
Flavor-Text:
The stalk this Pokémon carries in its wings serves as a sword to cut down opponents.
In a dire situation, the stalk can also serve as food.

Name: Lickitung
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in.
The itch won’t go away, either.

Name: Lickilicky
Effects: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Lickilicky’s strange tongue can stretch to many times the length of its body.
No one has figured out how Lickilicky’s tongue can stretch so far.

Name: Igglybuff
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text:
Its body has a faintly sweet scent and is bouncy and soft.
If it bounces even once, it cannot stop.

Name: Teddiursa
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text:
Its paws are soaked in oodles of honey.
When nervous, Teddiursa will lick its paws and soon have a smile back on its face.

Name: Ursaring
Flavor-Text: It usually wears a hardened expression, but when it’s licking up honey—which it loves—the joy it feels will cause it to break into a wide grin.

Name: Miltank
Effects:
Until this Pokémon leaves the Active Spot, this Pokémon's Rolling Frenzy attack does +30 damage.
This effect stacks.
Flavor-Text: Miltank produces highly nutritious milk, so it’s been supporting the lives of people and other Pokémon since ancient times.

Name: Azurill
Effects: Heal 50 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Its tail bounces like a rubber ball.
It flings that tail around to fight opponents bigger than itself.

Name: Swablu
Flavor-Text:
It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings.
It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

Name: Zangoose
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
It’s Seviper’s archrival.
To threaten those it encounters, it fans out the claws on its front paws.

Name: Fletchling
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is normally calm, but once it enters battle, its hormonal balance changes and it becomes aggressive.

Name: Inflatable Boat
Description: The Retreat Cost of the [W] Pokémon this card is attached to is 1 less.

Name: Memory Light
Description: The Pokémon this card is attached to can use any attack from its previous Evolutions. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

Name: Whitney
Description: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Miltank, and it recovers from being Asleep, Paralyzed, and Confused.

Name: Traveling Merchant
Description: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck. Put all Pokémon Tool cards you find there into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

Name: Morty
Description: For each of your [P] Pokémon in play, look at that many cards from the top of your opponent's deck and put them back in any order.

Name: Milotic
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may heal 60 damage from 1 of your [W] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Milotic has provided inspiration to many artists.
It has even been referred to as the most beautiful Pokémon of all.

Name: Stunfisk
Flavor-Text: Thanks to bacteria that lived in the mud flats with it, this Pokémon developed the organs it uses to generate electricity.

Name: Yamper
Flavor-Text:
This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats.
As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

Name: Latios
Ability-Effect: If you have Latias in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
It understands human speech and is highly intelligent.
It is a tender Pokémon that dislikes fighting.

Flavor-Text:
This Pokémon lives and nests on a riverbank.
After playing in the mud, it won’t be able to settle down unless it washes its body.

Effects: Heal 50 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Its tail bounces like a rubber ball.
It flings that tail around to fight opponents bigger than itself.

Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [R] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Morty
Description: For each of your [P] Pokémon in play, look at that many cards from the top of your opponent's deck and put them back in any order.

Name: Jumpluff ex
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Entei ex
Ability-Effect: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw a card.
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [R] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Raikou ex
Ability-Effect: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw a card.
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Poliwrath ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [W] Energy attached, this attack does 40 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Suicune ex
Ability-Effect: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw a card.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Chimchar
Flavor-Text:
Its fiery rear end is fueled by gas made in its belly.
Even rain can’t extinguish the fire.

Name: Monferno
Flavor-Text: It skillfully controls the intensity of the fire on its tail to keep its foes at an ideal distance.

Name: Psyduck
Effects: Your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand during their next turn.
Flavor-Text:
It is constantly wracked by a headache.
When the headache turns intense, it begins using mysterious powers.

Name: Golduck
Flavor-Text: When it swims at full speed using its long, webbed limbs, its forehead somehow begins to glow.

Name: Krabby
Flavor-Text:
It can be found near the sea.
The large pincers grow back if they are torn out of their sockets.

Name: Kingler
Effects:
Flip 2 coins.
If both of them are heads, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
Its large and hard pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength.
However, being so big, it is unwieldy to move.

Name: Pyukumuku
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 50 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
It lives in warm, shallow waters.
If it encounters a foe, it will spit out its internal organs as a means to punch them.

Name: Gible
Flavor-Text:
It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them.
The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.

Name: Gabite
Flavor-Text:
In rare cases, it molts and sheds its scales.
Medicine containing its scales as an ingredient will make a weary body feel invigorated.

Name: Paldean Wooper
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text:
After losing a territorial struggle, Wooper began living on land.
The Pokémon changed over time, developing a poisonous film to protect its body.

Name: Infernape ex
Effects: Discard all [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Mew ex
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Garchomp ex
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Paldean Clodsire ex
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 60 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Mantyke
Effects: Take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Basic Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
It’s highly friendly and easily tamed.
Tours that take people swimming with Mantyke are a super-popular beach activity.
